data.description:
## Context
bridge-inbound.py currently processes all inbound Jira events and writes them to the local ticket system. It needs a guard to skip writing events for tickets that are locally deleted (status == 'deleted' or archived == True), so Jira cannot resurrect a locally deleted ticket.

## What to do
In `plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-inbound.py`, find the per-ticket processing loop and add a guard before writing any events:

```python
# Check local status before processing inbound events
local_state = get_local_compiled_state(ticket_id, tracker_dir)
if local_state:
    local_status = local_state.get("status", "")
    local_archived = local_state.get("archived", False)
    if local_status == "deleted" or local_archived:
        logger.debug(
            "[inbound-bridge] skipping ticket %s: locally deleted or archived",
            ticket_id
        )
        continue  # or return early
```

## Files to modify
- `plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-inbound.py` — add deleted/archived guard in per-ticket processing

## Acceptance criteria
- T1 test (4297-3031) for inbound guard passes after this change
- No events are written to a locally-deleted ticket's directory during inbound sync
- A debug-level log entry is emitted when the guard fires
- Existing inbound bridge tests pass (non-breaking — the guard only adds a skip path)

## Depends on
T1 (4297-3031)

## Testing mode
UPDATE — adding guard to existing inbound bridge handler
data.title: Add inbound guard in bridge-inbound.py to skip locally-deleted tickets
